content.text:
Perfect! Now I can clearly see the bonds around atom C:15. Let me trace all the covalent connections from C:15 (the carbon atom in the bottom part of the fused ring system).

Looking at the zoomed view, I can identify the neighbour atoms of C:15:

1. **C:14** - The carbon atom above and to the left of C:15 (part of the fused ring)
2. **C:16** - The carbon atom to the right of C:15 (part of the fused ring)
3. **H:28** - The hydrogen atom attached below C:15

These are all the covalently bonded neighbours of C:15. Carbon typically forms 3 bonds in an aromatic ring system (2 with neighbouring carbons in the ring + 1 with hydrogen).

## Summary

**Neighbour atoms of C:15 (atomic index 15):**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|--------------|---------|
| 14 | C |
| 16 | C |
| 28 | H |

**geomconf_index: 694**

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 15, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
